frameworks.name: representational geometry
frameworks.one_line: Formalises how task information is structured and trades off generalisation versus flexibility

frameworks.name: representational compression
frameworks.one_line: Compression of task representations across learning underlies flexibility impairment

frameworks.name: artificial neural network modelling
frameworks.one_line: Networks parallel human task learning to expose representational dynamics

frameworks.name: auxiliary reconstruction loss
frameworks.one_line: Added objective maintains higher dimensionality to mitigate flexibility loss

core_question: Does representational compression of task representations across learning explain reduced cognitive flexibility?
key_assumptions: Pretraining length controls the degree of representational compression
Humans and artificial neural networks share comparable representational dynamics
manipulations: Pretraining length varied to control compression
Extra-dimensional shifting task across humans and ANNs
Added auxiliary reconstruction loss to network training
key_findings: Longer pretraining was associated with decreased flexibility in both humans and ANNs
Greater compression incurred a higher representational reorganisation cost
Reorganisation cost limited flexibility in network dynamics
Auxiliary reconstruction loss maintained higher dimensionality and mitigated flexibility impairment
interpretations: Representational compression across learning is the mechanism behind the flexibility impairment
Maintaining representational dimensionality preserves flexible adaptation to new contexts
